ocr.text: The memory market has experienced significant fluctuations over the past year, influenced by a combination of demand
dynamics and supply chain constraints. As illustrated in the accompanying data table, the Memory pricing trends reflect a year-
on-year growth, with a remarkable YoYChange(%) of 10% as of Q32023.
Amount($M)  YoYChange(%)  MarketShare(%)  Q32023  Q32022  Q32023
This growth is indicative of a recovering market, where pricing strategies have adapted to increased consumer demand and a
gradual easing of previously imposed supply chain disruptions. Notably, the Amount($M) for memory products reached an
impressive 300 in Q32023, showcasing a substantial increase from the previous year, where it was recorded at 0 in Q32022.
This stark contrast underscores the resurgent nature of the market, particularly as companies ramp up production capabilities to
meet rising needs across various technology sectors. Furthermore, TSMC's MarketShare(%) within this segment has solidified
at 25, positioning the company as a key player in the North American semiconductor landscape. The data highlights not only the
financial aspects but also the strategic importance of memory products in the broader context of the electronics industry, which
continues to evolve rapidly in response to technological advancements and consumer behavior. As we look ahead, monitoring
these trends will be crucial for stakeholders aiming to navigate the complexities of the semiconductor market effectively.
